task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the required CDC and WHO surveillance websites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser open with tabs or address bar showing URLs for CDC FluView, CDC MMWR, WHO Disease Outbreak News, or CDC NNDSS.

task_completion.description: Agent created a bookmark folder named 'Disease Surveillance'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox bookmarks toolbar, sidebar, or library window showing a folder explicitly named 'Disease Surveillance'.

task_completion.description: Agent added at least 5 bookmarks to the 'Disease Surveillance' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Disease Surveillance' bookmark folder is shown open or expanded, displaying a list of at least 5 saved web pages from the required CDC and WHO sources.

task_completion.description: Agent created the required JSON file in the Documents directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, VS Code) or terminal showing a file named 'surveillance_briefing.json' being edited or saved in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.

task_completion.description: JSON file contains required influenza and MMWR data structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file content shows 'influenza' and 'mmwr_latest' keys with populated sub-fields (source_url, surveillance_week, ili_activity_summary, title, publication_date, summary).

task_completion.description: JSON file contains required WHO outbreak news and notifiable diseases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file content shows 'who_outbreak_news' (with source_url, title, date, disease, location, summary) and 'notifiable_diseases' containing an array of at least 3 distinct diseases.

integrity.description: Agent used the graphical web browser to find information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Firefox being actively used to navigate and read the CDC/WHO websites, rather than relying solely on terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: Agent used the browser's UI to create bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Firefox bookmark dialog (star icon) or Library window being used to save the pages, rather than modifying the sqlite database via terminal.

integrity.description: Data in the JSON matches the websites visited.
integrity.visual_evidence: The information typed into the JSON file (dates, summaries, disease names) appears to correspond to the actual text visible in the browser screenshots of the CDC and WHO websites, not hallucinated or placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must visit cdc.gov and who.int. It must create a Firefox bookmark folder exactly named 'Disease Surveillance' containing at least 5 bookmarks. The final output must be a JSON file located at '/home/ga/Documents/surveillance_briefing.json' containing specific keys: briefing_date, influenza, mmwr_latest, who_outbreak_news, and notifiable_diseases (which must be an array of at least 3 items).
